title: Speculative Execution
summary: Explains speculative execution: executing all branch outcomes simultaneously, keeping results in temporary registers, and committing or discarding based on the actual outcome.
content_points: All possible branch outcomes executed in parallel before the condition is evaluated.
Results stored in temporary registers (not committed to architectural state).
On resolution: commit correct result; discard wrong-path results.
100% 'accuracy' but requires many extra registers; exponential growth if branches contain branches.
Simpler application: selection adder (computes both carry-in=0 and carry-in=1 sums simultaneously).
learning_objectives: Describe speculative execution and its implementation
Explain the register requirements and exponential cost problem
